message:
feat(lifecycle): graceful goroutine shutdown across all subsystems, checkpoint restore via respawn-pane, test reliability improvements

Graceful goroutine shutdown:
Multiple subsystems had fire-and-forget goroutines where Stop()/Close()
signaled quit but never waited for completion. Every subsystem now uses
sync.WaitGroup tracking so Stop()/Close() blocks until in-flight work
finishes:
- Coordinator: replaced stopOnce with explicit started/stopping flags +
  wg.Wait(). Double-Start now returns error. monitorLoop and digestLoop
  both call wg.Done()
- AttentionFeed: added stopWg so Stop() waits for heartbeat loop to
  finish delivering
- Scheduler: added lifecycleMu to serialize concurrent Start/Stop.
  Stats() reads atomic counters safely instead of copying struct (which
  raced on atomic fields)
- AutoScanner: added generation-based scan tracking (scanGeneration) so
  stale scan results from superseded scans never overwrite latest.
  Stop() waits via scanWG. Scanner function now injectable for testing
- TimelinePersister: replaced separate activeCheckpoints/checkpointStop
  maps with single checkpointRunner struct with done channel.
  StopCheckpoint() and Stop() both wait on <-runner.done
- Cache: Close() now waits for cleanup goroutine via cleanupWg
- Watcher: Close() waits for both poll/fsnotify loop (wg.Wait()) and
  in-flight debounced handler deliveries (via deliveryCond condition
  variable)

Checkpoint restore reliability:
Pane relaunch rewritten from SendKeysForAgent (typing into shell prompt,
prone to lost-input races) to respawn-pane -k (directly replaces pane
process). New relaunchRestoredPane retries up to 3x and verifies pane
runs expected command via waitForPaneCommand. Includes shell tokenizer
(nextShellToken) to parse past env prefixes, exec, and quoted paths.

Test improvements:
- Every graceful-shutdown fix has a corresponding test proving Stop()
  blocks until in-flight work finishes
- PrintHelp refactored to RenderHelp() string, eliminating ~10 tests
  that captured os.Stdout via pipe (data race source)
- Serve tests switched from file-backed SQLite to :memory:
- Latency regression tests skip under race detector
- SuccessCheck test made serial to avoid stdout races

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+//go:build race
+
+package robot
+
+func init() {
+	raceDetectorEnabled = true
+}

@@ -8,6 +8,7 @@ import (
 	"encoding/json"
 	"errors"
 	"fmt"
+	"io"
 	"os"
 	"os/exec"
 	"path/filepath"
@@ -1977,8 +1978,8 @@ Use --all to include the user pane (index depends on tmux pane-base-index).
 	},
 }
 
-// PrintHelp outputs AI agent help documentation
-func PrintHelp() {
+// RenderHelp returns AI agent help documentation.
+func RenderHelp() string {
 	var builder strings.Builder
 	builder.WriteString(`ntm (Named Tmux Manager) AI Agent Interface
 =============================================
@@ -2093,7 +2094,12 @@ Tips for AI Agents:
 For complete API documentation: docs/robot-api-design.md
 For machine-readable schema:    ntm --robot-capabilities
 `)
-	fmt.Println(builder.String())
+	return builder.String()
+}
+
+// PrintHelp outputs AI agent help documentation.
+func PrintHelp() {
+	_, _ = io.WriteString(os.Stdout, RenderHelp())
 }
 
 func renderHelpCommandSection(section robotHelpSection) string {

@@ -5,12 +5,9 @@ import (
 	"encoding/json"
 	"net/http"
 	"net/http/httptest"
-	"os"
-	"path/filepath"
 	"strings"
 	"testing"
 
-	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/events"
 	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/state"
 )
 
@@ -24,33 +21,7 @@ import (
 // setupValidationTestServer creates a server with store for validation tests.
 func setupValidationTestServer(t *testing.T) (*Server, *state.Store) {
 	t.Helper()
-
-	tmpDir := t.TempDir()
-	dbPath := filepath.Join(tmpDir, "test.db")
-
-	store, err := state.Open(dbPath)
-	if err != nil {
-		t.Fatalf("Failed to open store: %v", err)
-	}
-
-	if err := store.Migrate(); err != nil {
-		t.Fatalf("Failed to migrate: %v", err)
-	}
-
-	t.Cleanup(func() {
-		store.Close()
-		os.Remove(dbPath)
-	})
-
-	eventBus := events.NewEventBus(100)
-
-	srv := New(Config{
-		Port:       0,
-		EventBus:   eventBus,
-		StateStore: store,
-	})
-
-	return srv, store
+	return setupTestServer(t)
 }
 
 // TestHandlerValidationMethodNotAllowed verifies METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED error codes.

@@ -38,11 +38,10 @@ import (
 func setupTestServer(t *testing.T) (*Server, *state.Store) {
 	t.Helper()
 
-	// Create temp database
-	tmpDir := t.TempDir()
-	dbPath := filepath.Join(tmpDir, "test.db")
-
-	store, err := state.Open(dbPath)
+	// Most serve tests only exercise handler logic and do not need a file-backed
+	// database. Use an isolated in-memory store to avoid repeated disk-backed
+	// open+migrate cycles across the large package test suite.
+	store, err := state.Open(":memory:")
 	if err != nil {
 		t.Fatalf("Failed to open store: %v", err)
 	}
@@ -53,7 +52,6 @@ func setupTestServer(t *testing.T) (*Server, *state.Store) {
 
 	t.Cleanup(func() {
 		store.Close()
-		os.Remove(dbPath)
 	})
 
 	eventBus := events.NewEventBus(100)
